Beginners.question: A population refers to:

options.A: A subset of elements selected for study
options.B: The complete set of all elements of interest
options.C: Only the data that have been collected
options.D: A measure calculated from sample data

explanation_wrong.A: A population is not a subset; a subset refers to a sample.
explanation_wrong.C: Collected data usually represents a sample, not the full population.
explanation_wrong.D: A calculated measure from sample data is a statistic, not a population parameter.

Beginners.question: A sample is best defined as:

options.B: A subset drawn from the population

explanation_wrong.A: The entire group being studied is the population, not the sample.
explanation_wrong.C: A parameter belongs to the population, not the sample.
explanation_wrong.D: Studying the entire population is called a census, not sampling.

Beginners.question: Which of the following is a population parameter?

explanation_wrong.A: Sample mean is a statistic, not a parameter.
explanation_wrong.B: Sample standard deviation is a statistic.
explanation_wrong.D: Sample proportion is a statistic.

Beginners.question: The arithmetic mean is most sensitive to:

explanation_wrong.B: The number of observations affects calculation but is not the main reason for sensitivity.
explanation_wrong.C: The median is more resistant to extreme values than the mean.
explanation_wrong.D: The mode is unrelated to sensitivity to extreme values.

Beginners.question: The median of a dataset is:

options.A: The value that occurs most frequently
options.B: The middle value when observations are arranged in order

explanation_wrong.A: The most frequently occurring value is the mode.
explanation_wrong.C: The average of all values is the mean.
explanation_wrong.D: The median is not always greater than the mean; it depends on skewness.

Beginners.question: Which measure of central tendency is most appropriate for nominal (categorical) data?

explanation_wrong.A: The mean is suitable for quantitative data.
explanation_wrong.B: The median works better for ordinal data.
explanation_wrong.D: The geometric mean is used for rates and ratios.

Beginners.question: A pie chart is most suitable for showing:

options.B: The relative share of different categories in a total
options.C: The spread of data around the mean

explanation_wrong.A: Trends over time are better shown using line graphs.
explanation_wrong.C: Histograms are used for continuous frequency distributions.
explanation_wrong.D: Scatter plots show relationships between two continuous variables.

Beginners.question: A bar chart is most suitable for showing:

explanation_wrong.A: Trends over time are shown using line graphs.
explanation_wrong.C: Pie charts are used to show parts of a whole.
explanation_wrong.D: Histograms are used for continuous data distribution.

Beginners.question: The mode of a dataset is:

options.B: The value that occurs most frequently
options.C: The middle value in the ordered list
options.D: The difference between the highest and lowest value

explanation_wrong.A: The average of all values is the mean.
explanation_wrong.C: The middle value is the median.
explanation_wrong.D: The difference between highest and lowest is the range.

Beginners.question: Standard deviation measures:

options.A: The central value of the data
options.B: The average deviation of observations from the mean
options.C: The middle position in the data

explanation_wrong.A: Central value is measured by mean or median.
explanation_wrong.C: Middle position is given by median.
explanation_wrong.D: Proportion of extreme values is not measured by standard deviation.

Beginners.question: The coefficient of variation is particularly useful when:

options.A: Comparing variability of datasets that have different units or different means
options.C: Only absolute dispersion needs to be compared

explanation_wrong.B: CV is not suitable for purely categorical data.
explanation_wrong.C: When means are similar, standard deviation is better for absolute dispersion.
explanation_wrong.D: CV becomes unreliable when the mean is close to zero or negative.

Beginners.question: A frequency distribution is mainly used to:

options.B: Organise and summarise large sets of data
options.D: Eliminate the effect of extreme values

explanation_wrong.A: Mode can be found without a frequency distribution.
explanation_wrong.C: Pie charts show parts of a whole, not primarily for summarising large datasets.
explanation_wrong.D: Frequency distributions organise data but do not remove extreme values.

Moderate.question: If the mean of 20 observations is 45, the sum of all observations is:

explanation_wrong.B: 2.5 would be the mean if there were only 10 observations with sum 25.
explanation_wrong.C: 35 has no relation to the given information.
explanation_wrong.D: 15 is unrelated to finding the sum from the mean.

Moderate.question: The median of the data set 3, 8, 15, 7, 12, 20, 5 when arranged in order is:

explanation_wrong.A: 8 is not the middle value in the ordered list.
explanation_wrong.C: 15 is the fourth value, not the median.
explanation_wrong.D: 10 is not present in the data.

Moderate.question: Which measure of central tendency is least affected by extreme values?

explanation_wrong.A: Mean is highly affected by extreme values.
explanation_wrong.C: Mode may not represent the typical value well.
explanation_wrong.D: Range is entirely determined by extreme values.

Moderate.question: If every observation in a dataset is multiplied by 4, the standard deviation of the new dataset will be:

options.B: Four times the original standard deviation
options.C: One-fourth of the original standard deviation

explanation_wrong.A: Standard deviation scales by the same constant.
explanation_wrong.C: Standard deviation increases when values are multiplied by 4.
explanation_wrong.D: Standard deviation becomes zero only if all values are equal.

Moderate.question: When there are extreme values in income data, which measure best represents 'typical' income?

explanation_wrong.A: Mean is pulled upward by extreme high values.
explanation_wrong.C: Mode may not represent typical income well.
explanation_wrong.D: Geometric mean is not suitable for income levels.

Moderate.question: In a positively skewed distribution:

explanation_wrong.A: Mean = Median = Mode occurs in symmetric distributions.
explanation_wrong.C: Mode > Median > Mean occurs in negatively skewed distributions.
explanation_wrong.D: This relationship does not hold in positively skewed distributions.

Moderate.question: The Lorenz curve is primarily used to depict:

options.B: Degree of inequality in a distribution (e.g., income or wealth)

explanation_wrong.A: Central tendency is measured by mean, median or mode.
explanation_wrong.C: Probability is studied under probability theory.
explanation_wrong.D: Sampling variability is studied under sampling distributions.

Moderate.question: The coefficient of variation is used to compare:

options.B: Relative variability across datasets with different means or units

explanation_wrong.A: When means are similar, standard deviation is better.
explanation_wrong.C: CV is not suitable for purely categorical data.
explanation_wrong.D: CV is useful when means differ.

Moderate.question: A frequency polygon is constructed by:

options.A: Joining the midpoints of the tops of the bars of a histogram

explanation_wrong.B: Plotting only highest frequency does not form a polygon.
explanation_wrong.C: Ogive is drawn using cumulative frequencies.
explanation_wrong.D: Pie chart is not related to frequency polygon construction.

Moderate.question: Quartiles divide the data into:

explanation_wrong.A: Quartiles divide data into four parts.
explanation_wrong.C: Deciles divide data into ten parts.
explanation_wrong.D: Percentiles divide data into hundred parts.

Moderate.question: If mean = 60, median = 55 and mode = 50, the distribution is:

explanation_wrong.A: Symmetric distributions have Mean = Median = Mode.
explanation_wrong.C: Negatively skewed distributions have Mean < Median < Mode.
explanation_wrong.D: Uniform distributions are symmetric.

Moderate.question: The Gini coefficient ranges between:

explanation_wrong.A: Correlation coefficient ranges between –1 and +1.
explanation_wrong.C: Gini coefficient cannot be negative.
explanation_wrong.D: Gini coefficient lies between 0 and 1.

Expert.question: The empirical relationship between mean, median and mode holds reasonably well for:

explanation_wrong.A: The relation holds reasonably well only for moderately skewed unimodal distributions.
explanation_wrong.C: In symmetric distributions, Mean = Median = Mode.
explanation_wrong.D: The empirical relation does not hold well in highly skewed or multimodal distributions.

Expert.question: Why is the mean preferred over the median in symmetric distributions?

options.B: It utilises every observation in the data
options.D: It is always equal to the mode

explanation_wrong.A: Ease of calculation is not the main statistical reason.
explanation_wrong.C: Median is unaffected by skewness, but that is not why mean is preferred in symmetric cases.
explanation_wrong.D: Mode is not relevant in this comparison.

Expert.question: When should the coefficient of variation be preferred over standard deviation?

options.A: When comparing relative variability across datasets with different means or units
options.B: When the data are measured in the same unit and have similar means
options.C: When only absolute dispersion is of interest
options.D: When the sample size is very small

explanation_wrong.B: When means and units are similar, standard deviation is sufficient.
explanation_wrong.C: Absolute dispersion comparison does not require CV.
explanation_wrong.D: CV is unreliable when mean is close to zero.

Expert.question: A higher value of the Gini coefficient indicates:

explanation_wrong.A: Higher Gini coefficient indicates greater inequality.
explanation_wrong.C: Gini coefficient does not directly measure average income.
explanation_wrong.D: Gini coefficient is a measure of inequality, not variability alone.

Expert.question: In a right-skewed income distribution, which measure best represents typical income?

explanation_wrong.A: Mean is pulled upward by extreme high values.
explanation_wrong.C: Mode may not represent typical income well.
explanation_wrong.D: Range is not a measure of central tendency.

Expert.question: The empirical relation Mean – Mode = 3(Mean – Median) is most reliable when:

options.B: The distribution is moderately skewed and unimodal
options.D: The sample size is very small

explanation_wrong.A: The relation does not hold well in highly skewed distributions.
explanation_wrong.C: Presence of many zero values affects the relation.
explanation_wrong.D: Small sample size is not the condition for the empirical relation.

Expert.question: Which graphical tool is best for comparing frequency distributions of two states?

options.B: Frequency polygons of both states on the same graph

explanation_wrong.A: Pie chart shows parts of a whole for one variable.
explanation_wrong.C: Ogive shows cumulative distribution.
explanation_wrong.D: Frequency polygon of one state does not allow comparison.

Expert.question: If the Lorenz curve lies exactly on the diagonal line of equality, it means:

explanation_wrong.A: Perfect inequality occurs when Lorenz curve is far from the diagonal.
explanation_wrong.C: High skewness is not directly shown by the diagonal position.
explanation_wrong.D: Variance being zero is an extreme case not represented by the diagonal.

Expert.question: Percentiles are useful because they:

options.A: Divide data into only four parts
options.B: Help understand the position of a value relative to others
options.C: Are useful only for categorical data
options.D: Reduce the influence of extreme values automatically

explanation_wrong.A: Quartiles divide data into only four parts.
explanation_wrong.C: Categorical data do not have meaningful percentiles.
explanation_wrong.D: Percentiles do not reduce the influence of extreme values.

Expert.question: Which measure of dispersion is most affected by extreme values?

explanation_wrong.A: Range uses only two values and ignores the rest.
explanation_wrong.C: Range is highly affected by outliers.
explanation_wrong.D: Range cannot be negative.

Expert.question: Why is the geometric mean preferred over arithmetic mean when calculating average growth rates over years?

options.A: It gives higher weight to recent years
options.C: It is always higher than arithmetic mean
options.D: It can handle negative growth rates better

explanation_wrong.A: Geometric mean does not give higher weight to recent years.
explanation_wrong.C: Geometric mean is usually lower than arithmetic mean for growth rates.
explanation_wrong.D: Geometric mean can handle negative growth rates in some cases but is not preferred for that reason.

Expert.question: A distribution with skewness = 0 and kurtosis = 3 is best described as:

options.C: Approximately mesokurtic and symmetric (close to normal)

explanation_wrong.A: Positive skewness has skewness > 0.
explanation_wrong.B: Negative skewness has skewness < 0.
explanation_wrong.D: Platykurtic distributions have kurtosis < 3.

Pro.question: The sum of deviations of observations from their mean is always:

explanation_wrong.A: Sum of deviations from mean is always zero.
explanation_wrong.B: Mean utilises every observation.
explanation_wrong.D: Assumed mean method is valid for grouped data.

Pro.question: In a right-skewed distribution, the relationship between mean and median is usually:

explanation_wrong.A: In right-skewed distributions, Mean > Median.
explanation_wrong.C: Mean is usually greater than median in right-skewed data.
explanation_wrong.D: Mode does not solely determine the relationship between mean and median.

Pro.question: The coefficient of variation becomes unreliable when:

options.B: The mean of the dataset is close to zero or negative
options.D: Comparing datasets measured in the same unit

explanation_wrong.A: Perfect symmetry does not make CV misleading.
explanation_wrong.C: Large sample size does not affect reliability of CV.
explanation_wrong.D: Same units do not make CV unreliable.

Pro.question: Which statement about the median is correct?

options.B: It is less efficient than the mean but more robust to extreme values
options.C: It is suitable only for categorical variables
options.D: It is affected by skewness only in symmetric distributions

explanation_wrong.A: Median ignores extreme values but loses efficiency by not using all information.
explanation_wrong.C: This is not true only for categorical variables.
explanation_wrong.D: The statement is not incorrect only in symmetric cases.

Pro.question: If two datasets have identical means and standard deviations but different degrees of skewness, which statement is correct?

options.B: Their shapes and policy interpretations may still differ substantially

explanation_wrong.A: Lorenz curves need not coincide if skewness differs.
explanation_wrong.C: Medians need not be identical.
explanation_wrong.D: Ranges need not be equal.

Pro.question: When is it more appropriate to focus on percentiles rather than mean and standard deviation?

options.A: When the distribution is perfectly symmetric
options.B: When the distribution is highly skewed or has extreme values
options.C: When the sample size is very large

explanation_wrong.A: Gini coefficient gives a single summary measure.
explanation_wrong.C: Perfectly symmetric distributions do not require percentile focus.
explanation_wrong.D: Very small samples make percentile estimation unreliable.

Pro.question: What is a key limitation of histograms and frequency polygons?

options.B: They can be constructed only for continuous data
options.C: They may give a misleading impression if class intervals are not chosen carefully
options.D: They always provide exact numerical precision

explanation_wrong.A: Histograms and polygons can display frequency distributions.
explanation_wrong.B: They can be used for both discrete and continuous data with proper class intervals.
explanation_wrong.D: They do not always provide exact numerical precision without reading values.

Pro.question: In NSSO consumption expenditure data (typically right-skewed), which measure is most appropriate for 'typical' household consumption?

explanation_wrong.A: NSSO consumption data is typically right-skewed.
explanation_wrong.C: Mode is often not very useful in such skewed distributions.
explanation_wrong.D: Mean is usually higher than median in right-skewed data.

Pro.question: A leptokurtic distribution is characterised by:

options.B: Heavier tails and sharper peak than normal distribution
options.C: Exactly the same shape as normal distribution

explanation_wrong.A: Leptokurtic distributions have heavier tails.
explanation_wrong.C: Kurtosis = 3 corresponds to mesokurtic (normal-like).
explanation_wrong.D: Higher kurtosis indicates higher probability of extreme values, not lower variability.

Pro.question: A major limitation of correlation coefficient is that:

options.A: It can measure non-linear relationships well
options.B: It measures only linear association and does not imply causation
options.C: It can be used only for time-series data

explanation_wrong.A: Correlation does not imply causation.
explanation_wrong.C: Correlation can exist without causation.
explanation_wrong.D: This limitation applies to all types of data, not only time-series.

Pro.question: The coefficient of variation is difficult to interpret when the mean is close to zero because:

options.A: Standard deviation can still be used reliably
options.B: It becomes highly sensitive to small changes in the mean
options.C: It becomes equal to standard deviation
options.D: It is unaffected by the value of the mean

explanation_wrong.A: Standard deviation can still be used but CV becomes unstable.
explanation_wrong.C: CV is different from standard deviation when mean is near zero.
explanation_wrong.D: CV is highly sensitive to the value of the mean when it is close to zero.

Pro.question: For a comprehensive descriptive analysis of regional economic disparities before regression modelling, the most appropriate combination is:

options.A: Only the mean and the range
options.B: Mean, median, measures of dispersion (including CV), Gini coefficient/Lorenz curve, and skewness
options.C: Only the mode and pie charts

explanation_wrong.A: Mean and range alone give incomplete picture of distribution and inequality.
explanation_wrong.C: Mode and pie charts are insufficient for disparity analysis.
explanation_wrong.D: Standard deviation alone does not capture shape or inequality.
